question.text: The auditor appointed for an AIF under the audit engagement letter performs which critical function?

answer.text: Daily monitoring of the fund's trading positions
Real-time valuation of unlisted portfolio companies
Annual audit of the fund's financial statements and expressing an opinion on whether they present a true and fair view
Approving the fund manager's investment strategy

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The auditor conducts an annual statutory audit of the fund's financial statements and provides an independent opinion on whether they present a true and fair view of the fund's financial position. Daily monitoring is operations, real-time valuation is the administrator/valuer's role, and strategy approval is not an audit function. **The Trap:** Real-time valuation is tempting because valuation is crucial for AIFs, but the auditor reviews valuations for reasonableness — they do not perform real-time valuations. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XII covers the role of auditors in maintaining market integrity through independent assurance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check the auditor's name in the PPM. A Big Four or reputable audit firm adds credibility. Also verify the audit report is unqualified — qualified opinions or emphasis of matter paragraphs warrant investigation.
